goal: Pagina de settings dividida con sidebar en secciones que organicen mejor los ajustes de la app
notes:
Assumptions:
- The settings UI is macOS-first and uses the Settings scene in MomentumApp.
- The existing TrackerSettingsView will be refactored into section views instead of duplicated.
- No new dependencies or tooling changes are allowed.
- Spanish copy is preferred for new labels to match existing UI.

items.title: Define SettingsSection enum and selection model for sidebar navigation
items.notes: Model section identifiers, labels, and icons in a central place.

items.title: Create SettingsSidebarView listing sections with selection binding
items.notes: Sidebar should be keyboard navigable and use existing typography styles.

items.title: Introduce SettingsShellView using NavigationSplitView and shared selection
items.notes: Shell hosts sidebar + detail content and centralizes window layout.

items.title: Refactor TrackerSettingsView into section-specific subviews
items.notes: Extract appearance/tracking/idle/exclusions/assignment/privacy into dedicated views.

items.title: Move save/cancel action bar into SettingsShellView and keep draft state shared
items.notes: Ensure actions apply across sections and dismissal clears theme preview.

items.title: Update Settings scene in MomentumApp to present SettingsShellView
items.notes: Adjust default window size for split view without breaking current behavior.

items.title: Add detail routing map from SettingsSection to section view content
items.notes: Single switch that returns the correct view for each section.

items.title: Design sidebar ordering and labels to match mental model of settings
items.notes: Order should prioritize tracking, privacy, and appearance.

items.title: Add section-level headers/intro text for clarity inside each detail view
items.notes: Short intro copy, consistent with Spanish UI tone.

items.title: Ensure AssignmentRulesView navigation still works inside split view
items.notes: Confirm NavigationStack scope so the push works in section detail.

items.title: Extract Appearance section into dedicated view file
items.notes: Keep theme preview binding intact.

items.title: Extract Tracking section into dedicated view file
items.notes: Retain automation permission info and slider behavior.

items.title: Extract Idle section into dedicated view file
items.notes: Keep idle threshold stepper + explanatory text.

items.title: Extract Exclusions section into dedicated view file
items.notes: Keep AppExclusionEditor, domain and file exclusion editors intact.

items.title: Extract Assignment Rules section into dedicated view file
items.notes: Preserve picker identifiers and navigation link identifiers.

items.title: Extract Privacy & Data section into dedicated view file
items.notes: Preserve confirmation dialogs and error alerts wiring.

items.title: Add a reusable SettingsSectionHeader view style
items.notes: Use for consistency across section detail views.

items.title: Align section spacing and form background styling with new split view
items.notes: Keep grouped form style and window background color consistent.

items.title: Add sidebar icons mapping to each section
items.notes: Use SF Symbols already available in the app.

items.title: Ensure default selected section is meaningful on first open
items.notes: Default should be the primary tracking-related section.

items.title: Add keyboard focus and accessibility labels for sidebar items
items.notes: Verify VoiceOver-friendly labels for section names.

items.title: Update Settings window min width/height to fit sidebar + content
items.notes: Use a slightly wider default to avoid clipping.

items.title: Add section-level previews for unselected sections when no data
items.notes: Optional empty state text or hints for quick understanding.

items.title: Update Documentation.docc to mention new settings layout
items.notes: Keep docs succinct and consistent with current Spanish UI.

items.title: Add small UI regression note to PRDs/Notes if needed
items.notes: Only if documentation expects a log entry for UX changes.

items.title: Verify no new dependencies or tooling changes were introduced
items.notes: Checklist item to keep changes aligned with repo rules.
